info.title: Onshape API - Task
info.description: Create, access, and modify Tasks and Action Items.

tags.name: Task
tags.description: Create, access, and modify Tasks and Action Items.

get.summary: Lists tasks assigned to the specified user
get.description: Returns a list of tasks assigneed to the userId specified in the request. Only company admins can view tasks that were not created by them and are not assigned to them.

post.summary: Create a new task in a draft state.
Update the task and its properties.

href.description: URI for current page of resources.

items.description: Array of items in the current page.

next.description: URI for next page of the resources if more are available.

previous.description: URI for previous page of the resources.

companyId.description: Id of the company that owns the task.

documentId.description: Id of a document to associate the task to.

itemParams.description: References to include in the task.

href.description: URI to fetch complete information of the resource.

viewRef.description: URI to visualize the resource in a webclient if applicable.

companyId.description: Use to transfer task ownership to the company.

deleteItemIds.description: References to remove from the task.

items.description: References to remove from the task.

itemParams.description: References to add to the task.

bodyType.description: Body type to reference from a task.

configuration.description: Configuration of reference. Used to restrict a reference to a specific configuration of an element.

documentId.description: Id of a document. Required to reference a document or anything contained within it.

elementId.description: Id of an element reference. Used when referencing an element.

elementType.description: Type of element reference. Options are 0 (Part Studio), 1 (Assembly), 2 (Drawing), 3 (Feature Studio), 4 (Blob), 5 (Application), 6 (Table), 7 (Bill of Materials),  8 (Variable Studio), or 9 (Publication Item).

mimeType.description: Mimetype of reference. Used when referencing blob elements.

partId.description: Deterministic Id of a part. Used when referencing parts.

revisionId.description: Id of a revision to reference from a task.

versionId.description: Id of a document version. Used when referencing the version itself or an element or part in it.

workspaceId.description: Id of a document workspace. Used when referencing the workspace itself or an element or part in it.

BTTaskItemParams.description: References to add to the task.

propertyId.description: Id of the property to set.

value.description: Value to set for the property. User property values must be a list of `{ "id": "idValue" }` maps.

href.description: URI to fetch complete information of the resource.

viewRef.description: URI to visualize the resource in a webclient if applicable.

propertyOverrideStatus.description: 0: Unknown | 1: Not computed | 2: Computed without override | 3: Computed with override | 4: Computed with subassembly overrides | 5: Overridden

publishedDate.description: The date of publication of workflow

usesExternalPlm.description: Whether the workflow connects to an external PLM service like Arena

BTPublishedWorkflowInfo.description: Captures information about a published workflow

scopes.OAuth2ReadPII: Application can read your profile information
scopes.OAuth2Write: Application can write to your documents
scopes.OAuth2Delete: Application can delete your documents and workspaces
scopes.OAuth2Purchase: Application can request purchases on your behalf
scopes.OAuth2Share: Application can share and unshare documents on your behalf
scopes.document.create: Atlas Application can create a document
scopes.document.edit: Atlas Application can edit a document
scopes.document.delete: Atlas Application can delete a document
scopes.document.read: Atlas Application can read a document
scopes.enterprise.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.read: Atlas Application can read from an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.edit: Atlas Application can edit an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.delete: Atlas Application can delete an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.member.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.read: Atlas Application can read the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.edit: Atlas Application can edit the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.delete: Atlas Application can delete a member from an enterprise
scopes.webhook.create: Atlas Application can create a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.edit: Atlas Application can edit a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.read: Atlas Application can read a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.delete: Atlas Application can delete a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.PLMIntegration: PLM automation can invoke limited operations

BasicAuth.description: Use Basic Authentication with API Keys (key as username and secret as password) to authenticate requests.
